steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Central Ideas and Details question. The skill is identifying the main idea of the passage. The decisive clue is the final sentence: cuttlefish “changed their striking position to match the 3D images, suggesting that their vision is more like that of falcons and horses than that of octopuses or squid.”

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Cuttlefish eyes are much more complex than those of falcons or horses.’: The passage never discusses eye anatomy or compares structural complexity; it compares vision type (3D vs. not 3D). Eye complexity is not the main idea. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Wardill’s team designed 3D glasses for cuttlefish.’: The glasses are a detail of the experimental method, not the main idea of the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Before completing their study, Wardill’s team thought that cuttlefish have 3D vision, but horses may not.’: This misrepresents the passage. Before the study, researchers assumed cuttlefish lacked 3D vision (like octopuses and squid); horses were already known to have 3D vision. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘The finding of Wardill’s team’s study suggests like falcons and horses, cuttlefish may also have 3D vision.’: This directly captures the passage’s main idea: the study’s finding that cuttlefish vision resembles that of 3D-capable animals. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
